title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg, drafted by the Mavericks as the No. 1 pick, grew up in Maine rooting for the Celtics.
Flagg expressed loyalty to the Mavericks, saying he loves being with the team and doesn't want anything else.
Flagg impressed as a rookie averaging 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds, with the Mavericks holding contract options through 2029.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after a 42-and-a-half week absence, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists.
The Celtics secured a comfortable 25-point win over the Mavericks in a nationally televised NBA Finals rematch.
Boston Celtics are in second place in the Eastern Conference, with Tatum back in time for the upcoming playoff run.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned for the Celtics with no minutes restriction after 10 months due to a torn Achilles.
Tatum scored 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists, showing some rust but contributing significantly in a 120-100 win.
Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 24 points, while Neemias Queta added 16 points and 15 rebounds in solid support.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins chose not to make major trades at the deadline, prioritizing future assets over short-term gains.
General Manager Don Sweeney emphasized sticking to the team's rebuilding plan, avoiding rent-a-player deals with high market prices.
Boston retains four first-round picks over the next two years, maintaining flexibility for future strategic moves.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots were interested in Raiders' Maxx Crosby, who is set to be traded to the Ravens.
Baltimore Ravens offered the Raiders two first-round picks, including their 2027 first-rounder, for Maxx Crosby.
New England has over $60 million in cap space for free agency despite missing out on Crosby.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Nikola Vucevic of the Boston Celtics fractured his right ring finger during a game against the Dallas Mavericks.
Vucevic will undergo a procedure for his injury and is expected to be out for about a month.
His absence impacts Celtics' front-court depth, with limited options beyond Neemias Queta and rookie Amari Williams.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum made his return to the Celtics' lineup against the Dallas Mavericks at TD Garden after a ruptured Achilles.
After missing 62 games, Tatum recorded two assists and two rebounds in his first game back this season.
The Celtics are currently second in the Eastern Conference with a 41-21 record, despite Tatum's injury absence.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears for a fifth-round draft pick, per ESPN.
Trade becomes official next week when the new NFL league year starts, addresses Bears' urgent need at center.
Patriots' cap space to increase by $5.7 million, potential in-house replacement could be Jared Wilson.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Rachel Weisz stars with John Slattery in Netflix's new series 'Vladimir,' exploring campus scandals.
'Hamnet,' a fictionalized Shakespearean drama, is now streaming on Peacock, featuring Jessie Buckley and Paul Mescal.
Jafar Panahi's 'It Was Just an Accident,' a critique of Iran's regime, is available on Hulu.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics starting lineup for the first time this season against the Mavericks.
Coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's journey and work ethic, expressing gratitude for his commitment post-surgery.
Tatum replaced Baylor Scheierman in the lineup, with no mention of a minutes restriction for his return.
